action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
Oh, the candy store is bright and grand,
With chocolate bars and gum in hand!
But if you eat a whole big pile,
Your tummy starts to feel a while...
Like a bouncy ball inside your chest,
That makes you feel a little best...
No, not best, but very full and tight,
So put the lollipop down tonight!

SECTION 2
Then comes the sugar crash, *whoosh* and *snap*,
Your energy goes *pop* right off the map!
You were running fast, you were zooming high,
Now you're staring at the wall, saying "Why?"
Your teeth start to itch and feel so sore,
Like tiny bugs are living on the floor!
The sugar monsters hide inside your smile,
And make your mouth a sticky, sugary mile!

SECTION 3
So swap the cake for an apple slice,
Or a crunchy carrot, oh so nice!
Your body loves the veggies green,
The healthiest snack you've ever seen!
No more wiggly tummy, no more crash,
Just happy energy, fast and dash!
Your brain will zoom like a rocket ship,
While the candy-eater takes a nap and slip!

SECTION 4
So listen close to this sweet advice,
Before your teeth start to get a nice...
Brown and crumbly, sticky, sweet decay,
And you have to go to the dentist's day!
So save the sweets for a special treat,
Not every single day, not every week!
Eat your fruit, drink your water too,
And watch your smile grow big and new!
action.label: response
